exemplar.title: To what extent does engagement with first-person shooter (FPS) games impact the reaction time of male students in grades 6-9 compared to those who primarily engage with non-FPS games?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does engagement with first-person shooter (FPS) games impact the reaction time of male students in grades 6-9 compared to those who primarily engage with non-FPS games?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Clear connection of personal gaming experience to investigation design
Sustained independent thinking in choice of topic and methodology
Personal initiative demonstrated through consent procedures and equipment management
weaknesses: Could expand the VR extension with concrete feasibility details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Personal Engagement fully meet the top descriptors: the report is consistently personalized, demonstrates significant independent thinking, and shows clear initiative. Therefore, the full 2 marks are justified.

strengths: Research question is relevant and largely focused with defined variables
Background draws on academic sources and links FPS gaming to cognition
Methodology includes detailed control-variable table and consent procedures
Ethical and safety considerations such as anonymisation and equipment hygiene are addressed
weaknesses: Engagement metric not quantified, equating genre preference to engagement
Background coverage is brief and partly anecdotal
Method lacks randomisation and test validity checks
Minor omissions in fatigue and web-latency discussions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands consistently fall in the mid-band (3â4), showing a generally sound but not fully exhaustive investigation plan. The student meets many descriptor points but exhibits limitations in focus and methodological rigour. A score of 3 reflects this solid yet improvable work.

strengths: Adequate raw data set with five trials per participant
Appropriate use of means, standard deviations, and t-test
Broadly valid interpretation of FPS gamersâ faster reaction times
weaknesses: No explicit treatment of measurement uncertainty or error
Mis-stated units and inconsistent notation in data tables
Incomplete raw data presentation for non-FPS group
Lack of effect-size calculation and test-assumption discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis performance sits in the mid-band. While data processing and interpretation are appropriate, inconsistencies in units, lack of uncertainty treatment, and incomplete data reduce the overall mark to 3.

strengths: Relevant conclusion supported by means and p-value
Some comparison to scientific literature on neuro-cognitive effects
Identification of methodological limitations such as sample size and equipment issues
Realistic suggestions for additional paradigms and advanced equipment
weaknesses: Conclusion lacks quantitative depth and alternative explanations
Discussion of limitations is brief and non-quantitative
Suggestions, while relevant, are not elaborated in actionable detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation consistently meets mid-band descriptors: conclusions and comparisons are present but not deeply justified; limitations and improvements are acknowledged but without full critical depth, warranting 3 marks.

strengths: Logical use of headings, tables, and figures enhances readability
Terminology is mostly appropriate with clear sectioning
weaknesses: Occasional unit labelling errors and typos hinder clarity
Redundancy and misplacement (e.g., full survey forms) distract from flow
Bibliography has duplicate entries and inconsistent formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report is generally understandable and well-structured, recurring formatting inconsistencies, misplaced content, and bibliography errors reduce the effectiveness of communication. A score of 2 reflects clear but flawed presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Apart from averaging five trials, there is virtually no treatment of measurement uncertainty (no mention of timing precision, web-latency, or repeatability error).  Minimal acknowledgement aligns with the 1-2 band; awarding 1 mark reflects the very limited consideration provided.

The student cites several peer-reviewed studies and attempts to link outcomes to neuro-cognitive literature, demonstrating some relevant comparison.  The discussion, however, is superficial and largely descriptive, matching the 3-4 descriptor.

Sections follow the required IA structure, and materials/method, data, analysis and evaluation are present.  Some redundancy and misplaced information exist, but overall organisation is sound, warranting 3 marks within the 3-4 band.

Suggestionsâbroadening age range, adding more RT paradigms, using advanced/VR equipmentâare realistic and linked to cited weaknesses, but they are not elaborated in detail.  Hence the strand aligns with âsome realistic and relevant suggestionsâ (3-4).

The research question is relevant and largely focused (gender, age range, game genre and dependent variable are all defined).  Minor imprecision remainsââengagementâ is equated to preferred genre rather than quantified hoursâso it does not merit the fully-focused 5-6 band, but it is stronger than a merely stated question.

Weaknesses such as limited sample, single RT test and equipment issues are identified with brief methodological commentary, showing some understanding but not a âclear, in-depthâ analysis.  This merits placement in the 3-4 band.

The method is generally suitableâpre-screening, two groups, five trials each, control tableâbut several key issues (small sample, unvalidated web test, uncontrolled prior screen use, absence of randomisation) limit rigour.  These limitations prevent a âhighly appropriateâ rating, yet the plan is more than âvery limitedâ, so 3 is justified.

Background information outlines the history and types of reaction time, cites several academic sources and links FPS gaming to cognitive adaptations.  Nevertheless, coverage is brief and some claims (e.g., effects on driving) are anecdotal.  The information is mainly appropriate but not exhaustive, thus mid-band (3-4).

Most content is relevant to answering the RQ, with limited digressions; the inclusion of full survey forms in the main body is slightly distracting yet does not obscure understanding.  The work remains largely concise and purposeful, meriting 3 marks.

The student correctly states that FPS gamers show faster RTs, references mean differences and statistical significance, and offers plausible cognitive explanations.  Interpretation is broadly valid but lacks depth about alternative explanations or effect size, so mid-band (3-4) is appropriate.

The report is generally easy to follow with logical headings, figures and tables; only occasional typographical and unit errors hinder clarity.  This corresponds to a clear presentation with minimal errors (3-4 band, 3 marks).

The student chooses a topic that derives directly from his own regular gaming practice and links it explicitly to his sporting life.  He designs and administers both a pre-screening questionnaire and the experimental test session himself, demonstrating noticeable initiative beyond teacher-provided material.  Throughout the report he comments on what personally motivated each design choice, showing sustained independent thinking; therefore the highest band is appropriate.

Means, standard deviations and an independent-samples t-test (p-value) are provided, indicating appropriate processing.  However, calculations are not shown and some units are mis-stated (milliseconds vs 0.294 ms).  These inconsistencies position the work within âappropriate but inconsistentâ (3-4).

The rationale is thoroughly personalised: the student explains how differences observed while playing with friends sparked curiosity and how faster reaction time could benefit his own basketball performance.  This establishes clear personal significance and curiosity rather than a generic justification, aligning with the top descriptor.

Raw data include five trials for 10 participants per group, giving 100 data points.  While relevant, the quantity is modest and below the announced target of 15 per group, so it is ârelevant but incompleteâ, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Evidence of personal input is present in the construction of the consent procedure, the selection of on-line software, the control-variable table and the self-managed test sessions.  Initiative is shown in producing forms, obtaining parental consent and disinfecting equipment between trials; these features satisfy the âclear evidenceâ criterion.

Ethical awareness is shown through consent forms, opt-out statements and anonymising data; safety is considered by disinfecting equipment and keeping conditions quiet.  Some minor omissions (no discussion of screen-time fatigue) mean full marks are unwarranted; the strand sits comfortably in the 3-4 band.

Subject terminology (e.g., independent/dependent variables, standard deviation, p-value) is used appropriately.  Minor mis-labelling of units and occasional informal language (âmuch more advanced equipmentâ) produce a few errors, but accuracy is generally acceptable for 3 marks in this band.

A clear conclusion summarises the main findings and is supported by the processed means and p-value.  Yet it does not fully address the extent of the effect quantitatively or acknowledge limitations within the conclusion itself, placing it in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The student effectively connects personal gaming experience to the choice of topic, demonstrating clear personal engagement and initiative in framing the investigation. This justification shows independent thinking and relevance to personal sporting interests.

The research question is relevant and mostly well-focused, defining genre and demographic; however, it equates engagement to genre preference without quantifying hours. Consider specifying a measurable engagement metric to improve focus.

The hypothesis identifies expected faster reaction times in shooter gamers but repeats the research question and lacks a clear causal mechanism explanation. Consider elaborating on theoretical rationale with specific cognitive models.

The discussion interprets standard deviation differences effectively; yet it overgeneralizes precision from SD without exploring measurement error. Consider integrating uncertainty analysis for robustness.
Linking findings to driving and coordination illustrates thoughtful application. To strengthen the evaluation, reference specific literature on cognitive transfer to real-world tasks for validation.
The p-value interpretation is accurate and grounded in statistical reasoning. Including test assumptions (normality, equal variances) would further demonstrate depth of statistical analysis.

The background overview introduces reaction time history and experiment types with citations; nonetheless, some claims (e.g., driving effects) are anecdotal. Strengthen by including peer-reviewed studies and critical evaluation of sources.

The methodology description outlines the use of an online simple reaction time test clearly. To enhance rigour, address potential web latency and validate test reliability with calibration or pilot data.

The controlled variables table addresses hardware consistency, but the document notes âSee Table Underâ redundantly. Integrate control variables and ensure all significant factors (e.g., internet delay) are listed explicitly.

The expanded table of controlled variables is thorough, covering screen brightness, location, and hand dominance, showing methodological awareness. This level of detail supports reliability in data collection.

The materials list is comprehensive, including disinfectant and data recording tools; however, the absence of timing calibration equipment or software version details may limit reproducibility. Consider adding these specifics.

The pre-test procedures demonstrate careful planning with participant screening and consent steps, indicating personal input and ethical foresight. This approach enhances validity and ethical compliance.
The sequential method is clear, but lacking randomisation of participant order. Introducing random assignment would reduce order effects and strengthen internal validity.

Consent and opt-out procedures are well described, demonstrating strong ethical awareness. To deepen the analysis, discuss potential screen fatigue or participant discomfort and mitigation strategies.
The raw dataset presents five trials for ten FPS gamers, providing a solid data foundation. Yet the target sample size was 15 per group; acknowledge this shortfall explicitly in methodology.
The graphical representation of FPS reaction times enhances data interpretation. Ensure axis labels include units and scales to avoid reader confusion and improve clarity.
The non-FPS raw data table is incomplete, showing only candidate 1. Include all participants or note missing entries to support comprehensive analysis.
The continuation of non-FPS reaction times completes the dataset. A summary table consolidating both groups could improve comparability and streamline interpretation.
The observations correctly note faster FPS responses, but using descriptive statistics (e.g., effect size) here would strengthen interpretation rather than a simple statement.
The processed data table shows means and standard deviations; however, units are mis-stated as âmsâ within values. Maintain consistent notation by indicating units only in the table header.
The statement confirms the hypothesis but is brief. Expand by discussing possible alternative explanations or limitations within this inference to show critical evaluation.

The report effectively outlines focus on the research question and appropriate test selection as strengths. For a more critical evaluation, assess whether motivation varied across participants and its impact.
Identifying sample size and parental consent limitations shows methodological awareness. Enhance this by quantifying the potential effect of reduced sample size on statistical power.
The suggestion to use advanced equipment is realistic; consider specifying purchasing or accessing a chronometer system for precise timing to make the recommendation more actionable.

The extension exploring VR for reaction testing is innovative and shows personal engagement. To develop this further, outline specific VR tasks, metrics, and feasibility considerations for implementation.
The bibliography includes relevant sources but contains duplicate entries and inconsistent formatting. Ensure MLA or APA style consistency and remove redundancies to enhance presentation.
The âBibliographyâ header is clear, but consider labelling citation style (e.g., APA) and ordering entries consistently. This will improve academic rigour and clarity of sources.
